title: Polish Beef Kidney Casserole
description: A hearty, mustardy Polish dish that combines the rich flavors of beef kidney with a crunchy topping. This recipe is sugar-free-friendly and perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner.
instructions: Season the beef kidney with salt and black pepper.
Sear the beef kidney in a large skillet over medium-high heat until browned on all sides.
Transfer the beef kidney to a sous-vide machine and cook at 130°F (54°C) for 4 hours.
Sauté the onion in the same skillet until softened.
Add the caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the Dijon mustard and cook for an additional minute.
Gradually add the flour and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Remove the beef kidney from the sous-vide machine and slice into thin strips.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the egg beaters, sour cream, and chopped parsley.
Add the sliced beef kidney to the bowl and stir in the sautéed onion mixture.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Combine the flour and sour cream mixture in a small bowl and sprinkle over the top of the casserole.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown.
